The '''crastinal tense''' in Aelaki refers specifically to events expected to occur tomorrow. It combines the prefix '''goki-''' with the evidential and modal suffixes of the [[Future|future tense]].

== Formation ==

The crastinal is formed by attaching the prefix '''goki-''' to the verb stem. The same prefix is used for the [[Hesternal|hesternal]] (yesterday), but the two are distinguished by their suffixes: the crastinal uses future evidentials and modals, while the hesternal uses past evidentials.

== Evidential and modal suffixes ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Category !! Suffix !! Meaning
|-
| Auditory/Reported || '''-rum''' || The speaker has heard or been told the event will happen tomorrow
|-
| Inferential || '''-mu''' || The speaker infers the event will happen tomorrow
|-
| Intentional || '''-ng''' || The speaker intends to perform the action tomorrow
|-
| Optative || '''-ya''' || The speaker wishes or hopes the event will happen tomorrow
|-
| Deliberative || '''-yam''' || The speaker is considering whether the event should happen tomorrow
|}

== Diachronic notes ==

The reported suffix '''-rum''' is historically a fusion of '''-ro''' (past auditory/hearsay) and '''-mu''' (inferential), reflecting the idea that future reports combine hearsay with inference.

== See also ==

* [[Hesternal]] -- "yesterday" (shares the '''goki-''' prefix)
* [[Future]]
* [[Hodiernal]]
* [[Past]]
* [[Present]]
* [[Mythic]]

[[Category:grammatical tenses]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
